ALL SPECIALTIES / SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGIST / MONTANA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Montana Speech-Language Pathologist License

Montana Speech-Language Pathologist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Speech-Language Pathologist applications in Montana run through Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official graduate transcripts - From a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score report - Score of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verification - CF completion documentation.
  • Background check - Criminal background check.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Montana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

  1. STEP 1

    Create a User Account

    Navigate to the Montana Department of Labor & Industry License Portal and register for a new user account if you don't already have one.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Start and Complete Online Application

    Log in to your License Portal account, select 'Apply for a License,' choose 'Speech-Language Pathologist' from the list of professions, and complete all sections of the online application form. Pay the required application fee.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Arrange for Official Transcripts

    Have your educational institution(s) send official transcripts, verifying a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology, directly to the Board.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Provide Praxis Scores or ASHA CCC-SLP

    Arrange for the Educational Testing Service (ETS) to send official Praxis examination scores directly to the Board, or provide verification of your Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC-SLP) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Submit Verification of Supervised Clinical Experience

    If you are not submitting ASHA CCC-SLP, ensure your clinical fellowship supervisor completes and submits the 'Verification of Supervised Clinical Experience (Form 2)' directly to the Board.

    Fee: $200 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$200

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
Phone
406-444-6880
Website
boards.bsd.dli.mt.gov/speech-language-pathologists-audiologists
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Speech-Language Pathologist renewal in Montana follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$100
Continuing education
30 contact hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Montana SLP license cost?

$200 initial. $150 biennial renewal.

Is Montana part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes.

Does Montana allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes.

Does Montana have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

Yes. Montana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Montana without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Montana?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$200

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years · $100

Licensing board

Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists

boards.bsd.dli.mt.gov/speech-language-pathologists-audiologists

406-444-6880

This page is maintained as a reference for speech-language pathologists and recruiters evaluating Montana assignments.
